 {"id":"bd-1wy6","title":"E2E Tests: Cost tracking across session lifecycle","description":"E2E Tests for Cost tracking across session lifecycle.

## Test Scenarios

1. Real-time cost display
   - Spawn session, send prompts
   - Verify cost updates in status
   - Verify per-agent breakdown

2. Budget alerts
   - Set budget threshold
   - Exceed threshold
   - Verify alert displayed
   - Verify continues (warn only)

3. ntm cost command
   - Run ntm cost myproject
   - Verify current session cost
   - Run ntm cost --history
   - Verify historical data

4. Multi-model accuracy
   - Spawn Claude + Codex
   - Send similar prompts
   - Verify different costs calculated correctly

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-COST] Agent AGENT: N tokens, $X.XXXX
[E2E-COST] Session total: $X.XX
[E2E-COST] Budget alert: $CURRENT > $THRESHOLD
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Real-time cost updates within 5 seconds
3. Budget alerts display when threshold exceeded
4. ntm cost command shows current and historical costs
5. Multi-model pricing applied correctly
6. Detailed logging with [E2E-COST] prefix
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify cost tracking
2. Costs tracked from spawn to kill
3. Per-agent costs accurate
4. Session totals correct
5. Cost export to file works
6.  {"id":"bd-1x93u","title":"Task: Add ensemble commands to --robot-capabilities schema","description":"# Task: Add ensemble commands to --robot-capabilities schema

Expose ensemble robot commands in the machine-readable capabilities output so other agents can discover them.

## Location
- internal/robot/capabilities.go (append to existing capabilities list)
- internal/robot/capabilities_types.go (if new struct fields needed)

## Commands to Include
- --robot-ensemble=SESSION
- --robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION
- --robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION
- --robot-ensemble-modes
- --robot-ensemble-presets

## Required Fields
For each command entry:
- name
- description
- flags (with types + defaults)
- examples
- notes on exit codes (0/1/2) where relevant

### Flag Coverage (must be documented)
- **Spawn:** `--question`, `--preset`, `--modes`, `--agents`, `--assignment`, `--allow-advanced`, `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`, `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`, `--project`
- **Modes:** `--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`
- **Synthesize:** `--strategy`, `--format`, `--output`, `--force`

### Tier Flags (must be documented)
- `--tier` (core|advanced|experimental|all)
- `--allow-advanced` where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Capabilities JSON includes all ensemble commands and flags
- Output remains deterministic (stable ordering)
- Arrays are initialized to [] (not nil)
- Existing capabilities tests updated to assert ensemble entries","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:09.376997724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:02:03.652779504Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:02:03.652710886Z","close_reason":"Added ensemble commands (modes, presets, synthesize, spawn) to --robot-capabilities schema with ensemble category","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x93u","depends_on_id":"bd-10gyo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x93u","depends_on_id":"bd-1mdym","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x93u","depends_on_id":"bd-75iqr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x93u","depends_on_id":"bd-rcwdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1x9x8","title":"Task: Prompt lint rule set + severities","description":"## Scope
Define the initial lint rules and their severities.

## Candidate Rules
- Oversized prompt (bytes + estimated tokens) → warning/error.
- Contains synthetic/real secret pattern (via redaction engine) → warning/block depending on mode.
- Contains destructive command patterns (`rm -rf`, `git reset --hard`, `git push --force`) → high severity; integrate DCG.
- Missing required context markers (optional, configurable).

## Output Schema
- `id` (stable string)
- `severity` (`info|warning|error`)
- `message`
- `help` (how to fix)
- `metadata` (counts, sizes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Rules are deterministic and cheap.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:25:41.388885072Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:25:41.400689591Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x9x8","depends_on_id":"bd-in4ac","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:25:41.400660096Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1xc00","title":"Task: Implement Command Kernel registry","description":"## Scope\nCreate internal/kernel with a command registry that drives CLI/TUI/REST/OpenAPI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Define Command struct (Name, Description, Category, Input/Output schemas, RESTBinding, Examples, SafetyLevel, EmitsEvents, Idempotent).\n- Registry supports register/list/find with deterministic ordering.\n- Validation: unique command names, REST path conflicts, required metadata (Examples for OpenAPI).\n- Export programmatic list for OpenAPI generator + command palette.\n\n## Logging\n- log/slog on register failures + conflicts (command name, path).\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: registry add/find/list, conflict detection, deterministic ordering.\n- Unit: validation errors surface expected codes.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Kernel registry compiles and is used by at least one CLI command.\n- Conflicts are detected at startup with actionable errors.\n- Tests pass with go test ./...\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:07.517444049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:28.602062971Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:32:28.602031532Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: kernel compiles, used by 10+ CLI commands, conflict detection works, tests pass (8/8)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1xm1c","title":"Task: E2E tests for Orchestration Features (batch, review-queue, rebalance, scale)","description":"## Overview
E2E tests for Fleet Management commands using VHS for visual recording and detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

### Scale E2E Tests
```bash
# Test: Scale up from 0 to N
test_scale_up_from_zero() {
    ntm spawn myproject --cc=0 --cod=0 --gmi=0
    ntm scale myproject --cc=3
    assert_pane_count myproject cc 3
}

# Test: Scale down from N to 1
test_scale_down_to_one() {
    ntm spawn myproject --cc=5
    echo "y" | ntm scale myproject --cc=1
    assert_pane_count myproject cc 1
}

# Test: Mixed scale (up and down together)
test_mixed_scale() {
    ntm spawn myproject --cc=3 --cod=3
    echo "y" | ntm scale myproject --cc=5 --cod=1 --gmi=2
    assert_pane_count myproject cc 5
    assert_pane_count myproject cod 1
    assert_pane_count myproject gmi 2
}

# Test: Dry-run shows correct plan
test_scale_dry_run() {
    output=$(ntm scale myproject --cc=10 --dry-run)
    assert_contains "$output" "Would spawn 7 Claude agents"
}

# Test: Force flag skips confirmation
test_scale_force() {
    ntm scale myproject --cc=1 --force
    # Should not prompt
}
```

### Rebalance E2E Tests
```bash
# Test: Suggestions for overloaded agent
test_rebalance_overloaded() {
    # Setup: cc_1 has 5 tasks, cc_2 has 1 task
    output=$(ntm rebalance myproject)
    assert_contains "$output" "Recommended Transfers"
}

# Test: Apply rebalance with confirmation
test_rebalance_apply() {
    echo "y" | ntm rebalance myproject --apply
    # Verify transfers occurred
}

# Test: Dead agent detection
test_rebalance_dead_agent() {
    # Kill an agent mid-session
    output=$(ntm rebalance myproject)
    assert_contains "$output" "DEAD"
    assert_contains "$output" "Orphaned tasks"
}
```

### Review Queue E2E Tests
```bash
# Test: Show idle agents with suggestions
test_review_queue_idle() {
    output=$(ntm review-queue myproject)
    assert_contains "$output" "Idle Agents"
    assert_contains "$output" "Suggested Assignments"
}

# Test: Send prompts after confirmation
test_review_queue_send() {
    echo "y" | ntm review-queue myproject --send
    # Verify prompts sent
}

# Test: Custom idle threshold
test_review_queue_threshold() {
    output=$(ntm review-queue myproject --idle-threshold 5m)
    # Only agents idle >5m should appear
}
```

### Batch Send E2E Tests
```bash
# Test: Basic batch send
test_batch_basic() {
    cat > prompts.txt << 'PROMPTS'
First task
---
Second task
PROMPTS
    ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt
}

# Test: Batch with confirmation for each
test_batch_confirm_each() {
    echo -e "y\ny\ny" | ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --confirm-each
}

# Test: Batch with delay
test_batch_delay() {
    start=$(date +%s)
    ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --delay 2s
    end=$(date +%s)
    assert_ge $((end - start)) 4
}
```

## VHS Recording Template
```tape
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Output scale_test.gif

Type "ntm scale myproject --cc=5 --dry-run"
Enter
Sleep 2s

Type "ntm scale myproject --cc=5"
Enter
Sleep 3s
Type "y"
Enter
Sleep 5s
```

## Logging Format
```bash
log_e2e() {
    echo "[E2E-FLEET] test=$1 session=$2 action=$3 result=$4 duration_ms=$5"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scale up from 0 works
- [ ] Scale down with confirmation works
- [ ] Mixed scaling works
- [ ] Dry-run shows accurate plan
- [ ] Force flag skips confirmation
- [ ] Rebalance detects dead agents
- [ ] Review queue sends prompts
- [ ] Batch processes in order
- [ ] All tests have VHS recordings
- [ ] Detailed logging throughout

## Dependencies
- VHS installed for visual recordings
- Real tmux session for pane operations
- br CLI for bead integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:30:54.495050692Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:14.634837362Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xm1c","depends_on_id":"bd-10ufs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xm1c","depends_on_id":"bd-jxs5b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-349x","title":"Task: Add webhook configuration in ntm.yaml","description":"## Overview
Implement configuration parsing for webhooks in ntm.yaml.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/webhooks.go:
   - WebhookConfig struct
   - ParseWebhookConfig(yaml []byte) ([]WebhookConfig, error)
   - ValidateConfig() error

2. Config structure:
   webhooks:
     - name: slack-notifications
       url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
       events:
         - agent.completed
         - agent.error
       formatter: slack
       filter:
         session: myproject*
         agent_type: [claude, codex]
         severity: [warning, error]
       retry:
         max_attempts: 5
         backoff: exponential
       timeout: 30s
       secret: ${NTM_WEBHOOK_SECRET}  # env var substitution

3. Validation rules:
   - URL must be valid HTTP(S)
   - Events must be recognized event types
   - Formatter must be registered
   - Warn on unrecognized options

4. Hot reload:
   - Detect config file changes
   - Reload webhooks without restart
   - Log config changes

## Implementation Notes
- Use mapstructure for YAML parsing
- Support environment variable substitution
- Glob patterns for session filtering

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config parses correctly from ntm.yaml
- Validation catches common errors
- Hot reload works
- Unit tests for config parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:50.948504334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:20.358287058Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-349x","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-34tnr","title":"Task: Implement findings deduplication engine","description":"## Implementation
Build deterministic similarity + clustering for findings:

1. Similarity function (no ML):
   - Normalized text overlap
   - Shared evidence pointers
   - RedundancyScore as a weighting factor

2. Clustering:
   - Stable cluster IDs
   - Deterministic ordering inside clusters
   - Merge produces a canonical finding while preserving provenance chain

3. CLI support hooks for `ntm ensemble dedupe --interactive/--auto`.

## Logging
- Log similarity scores + cluster sizes + merge decisions

## Tests
- Unit: similarity edge cases, cluster determinism
- Integration: merge preserves provenance + citations
- E2E: CLI auto dedupe produces stable output

## Dependencies
- OutputSchema (bd-1wnxl)
- Provenance tracking (bd-364w3)
- Redundancy normalization (bd-254do)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:41:38.293805641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:44:38.284448488Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:44:38.284362788Z","close_reason":"Implemented findings deduplication engine with: deterministic similarity function (normalized text + evidence pointers), clustering with stable IDs, canonical finding selection (prefer high confidence), provenance tracking. Added comprehensive unit tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-34tnr","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34tnr","depends_on_id":"bd-254do","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34tnr","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-34tnr","depends_on_id":"bd-u91ot","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3518e","title":"Unit Tests: Dependency awareness and auto-reassignment logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Test the dependency-aware assignment and auto-reassignment on blocker completion.

## Test Cases

### Filtering Blocked Beads
- Filter out beads with non-empty blocked_by
- Include only actionable beads
- Handle bead with multiple blockers
- Handle bead with single blocker

### Unblock Detection
- Detect when blocker bead is closed
- Identify all beads unblocked by completion
- Handle chain unblocking (A blocks B blocks C)
- Handle multiple beads unblocked simultaneously

### Auto-Reassignment
- Queue newly unblocked beads
- Assign to first idle agent
- Respect strategy for reassignment
- Handle no idle agents (queue only)

### Edge Cases
- Circular dependencies (should be filtered)
- Bead completes but no new unblocks
- All agents busy when unblock happens

## Logging Requirements
- Log each filter decision
- Log unblock detection events
- Log reassignment decisions

## Test File
tests/dependency_awareness_test.go","notes":"Created /dp/ntm/tests/dependency_awareness_test.go with 22 comprehensive unit tests:

## Section 1: Filtering Blocked Beads (4 tests)

## Section 5: State Transitions (2 tests)
- TestStateTransition_FailedToAssigned
- TestStateTransition_CompletedCannotRetry

## Section 6: JSON Envelope Format (2 tests)
- TestJSONEnvelope_UnblockedBeadFields
- TestJSONEnvelope_SkippedItemReason

All 22 tests pass. Tests use internal/assignment and internal/bv packages.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:39:19.213541744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:50:39.544093873Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:50:39.543993864Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3518e","depends_on_id":"bd-264n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3518e","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-351ir","title":"Task: Apply redaction to ntm send + --robot-send","description":"## Scope
Apply redaction safety to outbound prompts.

## Behavior
- Before sending to panes, scan the prompt.
- If mode = `warn`:
  - emit a warning to stderr (human) or a `warnings[]` field (robot responses where applicable)
  - send prompt unchanged
- If mode = `redact`:
  - replace sensitive substrings before sending
  - show a summary of what was redacted (categories count)
- If mode = `block`:
  - abort send unless `--allow-secret`

## Robot Mode
- Ensure `--robot-send=SESSION` returns stable fields and preserves existing conventions:
  - `success` bool
  - `timestamp` RFC3339
  - `blocked` bool
  - `redaction` summary object (counts by category)
  - `warnings[]`
  - `error_code = "SENSITIVE_DATA_BLOCKED"` when blocked

## Acceptance Criteria
- No raw secret substrings appear in tmux panes when redact/block is enabled.
- Human UX makes it obvious what happened and how to override.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.463481809Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:15:05.557929300Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-351ir","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.476557559Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-351ir","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.485367639Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}
+{"id":"bd-351ir","title":"Task: Apply redaction to ntm send + --robot-send","description":"## Scope
Apply redaction safety to outbound prompts.

## Behavior
- Before sending to panes, scan the prompt.
- If mode = `warn`:
  - emit a warning to stderr (human) or a `warnings[]` field (robot responses where applicable)
  - send prompt unchanged
- If mode = `redact`:
  - replace sensitive substrings before sending
  - show a summary of what was redacted (categories count)
- If mode = `block`:
  - abort send unless `--allow-secret`

## Robot Mode
- Ensure `--robot-send=SESSION` returns stable fields and preserves existing conventions:
  - `success` bool
  - `timestamp` RFC3339
  - `blocked` bool
  - `redaction` summary object (counts by category)
  - `warnings[]`
  - `error_code = "SENSITIVE_DATA_BLOCKED"` when blocked

## Acceptance Criteria
- No raw secret substrings appear in tmux panes when redact/block is enabled.
- Human UX makes it obvious what happened and how to override.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.463481809Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:05:49.788068861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T03:05:49.788004311Z","close_reason":"Redaction fully integrated into ntm send and --robot-send. Tests passing: warn/redact/block modes work, proper error codes, schema stability verified.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-351ir","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.476557559Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-351ir","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:21:57.485367639Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}
 {"id":"bd-352n","title":"Task: Add help --minimal / --full switch","description":"## Overview
Replace tiered/proficiency gating with a simple help verbosity switch.

## Deliverables
1. Add --minimal and --full flags to ntm help (or ntm --help).
2. Minimal view shows essential commands only (spawn, send, status, kill, help).
3. Full view shows the complete command set (current behavior).
4. Optional config default: help_verbosity = minimal|full.

## Non-Goals
- No proficiency tiers, gating, or promotion logic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal help is concise and stable
- Full help remains unchanged
- No command gating","notes":"Tiered gating removed; this task is the canonical help verbosity switch.

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-352u","title":"Task: Add timeline persistence for post-session analysis","description":"## Overview
Enable saving timeline data to disk for analysis after session ends.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/state/timeline_persist.go:
   - SaveTimeline(sessionID string, events []AgentEvent) error
   - LoadTimeline(sessionID string) ([]AgentEvent, error)
   - ListTimelines() ([]TimelineInfo, error)

2. Storage format:
   - JSONL file per session: ~/.local/share/ntm/timelines/<session>.jsonl
   - Each line is one AgentEvent as JSON
   - Include header with session metadata

3. Automatic persistence:
   - Save on session end
   - Periodic checkpoint every 5 minutes
   - Recover from checkpoint on crash

4. CLI commands:
   - ntm timeline show <session-id> - view historical timeline
   - ntm timeline list - list available timelines
   - ntm timeline delete <session-id> - remove old timelines

5. Retention policy:
   - Keep last 30 sessions by default
   - Configurable via ntm.yaml
   - Automatic cleanup of old timelines

## Implementation Notes
- Use standard JSON encoding for portability
- Compress old timelines with gzip
- Index by session ID for fast lookup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timelines persist across NTM restarts
- Can load and view historical timelines
- Automatic cleanup works
- Unit tests for persistence logic","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyCliff","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:33.281526043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T04:19:39.965551811Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-352u","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
